title: Europa ajunge la antipozi. De ce ar trebui să ne pese
summary: The EU signed a free trade and strategic partnership agreement with Australia after 8 years of negotiations, driven by Trump's disruptive trade policies. The deal eliminates 98% of Australian export tariffs, includes defense and security cooperation, and represents the EU's awakening as a geopolitical actor building alternative partnership networks.
talking_points: The EU-Australia trade deal was unblocked not by commercial revelation but by Trump turning the global economy into chaos, forcing cooperation
The agreement neutralizes Beijing's economic leverage by securing access to Australian lithium, aluminum, and manganese critical for batteries and green transition
Von der Leyen's speech in Australian parliament signaled a fundamental shift in EU strategic thinking, treating European security and Indo-Pacific stability as interconnected
The EU suffers from a catastrophic communication deficit that allows extremist narratives to fill the vacuum about why Europe matters
Romania benefits automatically but contributes to the problem by sending disengaged MEPs who treat Brussels positions as sinecures
author: Andrei Colțea
